meta.title: Fujian
meta.subtitle: Industry-cluster overview
meta.tagline: Fujian is a coastal chain of one-product county-cities — sneakers, porcelain, stone and tea — wedged between the Pearl River and Yangtze deltas, with new world-beaters in batteries and auto glass. This is the cluster map of the province.
meta.methodology: Coordinates are from Google Places, converted from China's GCJ-02 datum to WGS-84 to sit correctly on the OpenStreetMap / CARTO basemap. Each pin marks a place's dominant manufacturing specialty drawn from current sourcing guides; clusters are indicative, not exclusive, and shift over time. The map covers Fujian's nine prefecture-level cities.

clusters.note: China's sneaker capital — Chendai makes 1 in 5 of the world's sneakers (Anta, Xtep, 361°); also snack-food and hygiene giants.
Casual and leisure clothing capital, with Asia's largest specialist clothing market; children's wear.
China's stone-processing capital (Shuitou) — granite and marble; plus plumbing hardware.
Stone-sculpture capital; also Dali Foods, a national snack-food giant.
Home of Tieguanyin oolong; also a rattan-iron crafts cluster.
Porcelain capital — Blanc de Chine white porcelain, figurines and ceramic crafts.
Incense (joss-stick) capital; lacquer and bamboo crafts.
Mass OEM footwear for global brands (and famous for replicas); also ~30% of the world's oil paintings and gold-jewelry processing.
Classical redwood and mahogany furniture and woodcarving capital.
Electronics, LED lighting, displays and machinery; sunglasses; a major port and special economic zone.
Canned food, mushrooms and fruit; food and beverage exports; also a clock and watch base.
Chemical-fiber and warp-knit textile capital — yarns and knitted fabric.
Fuyao Glass, the world's largest auto-glass maker; food, plastics and electronics.
Provincial capital — optoelectronics, displays (BOE) and textile headquarters.
CATL, the world's largest EV-battery maker; plus one of the world's biggest stainless-steel bases.
Fuding white-tea capital.
Construction machinery — Longgong loaders and forklifts; heavy equipment.
Zijin Mining, a gold and copper giant; copper processing.
Sangang steel and a fluorine-chemicals base; forestry.
Wuyi rock tea and Da Hong Pao — the oolong heartland.
Bamboo products, forestry and paper.

orientation.v: Footwear (Jinjiang), clothing (Shishi), stone (Nan'an), porcelain (Dehua), tea (Anxi) — the densest cluster county in the province.
The coast (Quanzhou, Xiamen, Fuzhou, Putian, Ningde) carries the export light industry; the inland west (Longyan, Sanming, Nanping) holds heavy industry, mining and tea.
Ningde is now a global new-energy hub — CATL batteries and a giant stainless-steel base.
Three of China's great teas: Tieguanyin (Anxi), Wuyi rock tea (Wuyishan) and white tea (Fuding).
Xiamen and Fuzhou are the ports and brand HQs; the surrounding counties are where things are made.

notes: Fujian's clusters cling to the coast — a chain of single-product county-cities between the Pearl River and Yangtze deltas, with heavy industry and tea inland to the west.
Quanzhou alone is one of China's densest cluster zones: sneakers in Jinjiang, clothing in Shishi, stone in Nan'an, porcelain in Dehua, oolong in Anxi.
The province pairs classic export light industry — shoes, clothing, ceramics, crafts — with new world number-ones: CATL batteries and Fuyao auto glass.
A pin marks a place's dominant specialty, not its only one; Quanzhou's counties in particular each make several things.
